[Red Bull League of its Own - What are your thoughts? - Reddit]: -While I wasn't there, the location looked really exciting. I loved how the stage and entrances were more like boxing matches, with players walking through the audience to get to a center stage.

-It was actually very nice to have not only an international event with T1 playing against LEC teams, but also with orgs like NNO/BIG/EINS who have big fanbases but rarely get the chance to play against the big teams like G2. I think it showcased that there is a lot of high quality gameplay outside of the standard top tier leagues.

-Excellent organization with top casters and presenters like Drakos/Vedius and Sjokz/Laure present, but also costreamers like Caedrel getting a booth in the arena. Really made the viewing experience online very exciting.

Cons: [...] Go to leagueoflegends   

r/leagueoflegends   • 

chf\_gang

# Red Bull League of its Own - What are your thoughts?

Now that Red Bull League of its Own has passed, I think we can all agree that it was a big success and we would all like to see more events like this in the future! However, the event didn't go without faults. I wanted to know what everyone thought about what worked well and some improvements for next years iteration.

I'll go first:

Pros:

-I loved the silly format. I really enjoyed how Red Bull took this opportunity to experiment with what is possible in the realm of eSports instead of sticking to the same formula that Riot uses for all their events. [...] Cons:

-Too many games. Or more particularly, the length of the event. Even fans inside the arena didn't stick around until the end. For T1 especially, you could tell they were getting kind of bored and tired by the end. It also felt really ridiculous how the caster had to hype them up and let them enter the arena multiple times.

-I would have enjoyed it if teams faced each other up and maybe the captains of the teams gave a handshake before sitting down at their PC. Seemed really weird how 1 team was already sitting down at their PCs and ignored the other team walking up to the stage.

So what do you guys think?

#### With HopEUm, Quickshot Wants to Rebuild Europe’s League Conversation

For years, European League of Legends fans had no shortage of opinions. What they lacked, increasingly, was a shared room to put them in. That is the space G2's new weekly live show, HopEUm, is trying [...] The event’s main draw is T1, the South Korean powerhouse led by veteran mid laner Lee “Faker” Sang-hyeok. After falling to G2 Esports in last year’s final in Berlin, T1 will face a field that includes Karmine Corp, Gentle Mates, NNO Old, and G2. The tournament also marks the in-person competitive debut of Los Ratones, a streamer-led squad captained by popular broadcaster Marc “Caedrel” Lamont.

Organizers expect more than 15,000 fans in attendance, with thousands more following the action online. This year’s format promises a fast-paced schedule, surprise role swaps and marquee matchups between esports rivals. An additional 1-on-1 showmatch, backed by Opel, is set to provide a mid-event highlight.

[Red Bull Struggling with Similar 'Correlation' Issue from 2024]: According to Horner, Red Bull's problems can be traced back to correlation issues between the team's wind tunnel testing and the car's actual performance on track.

More News: Max Verstappen Relieved with Pole Position: "It Wasn't Easy"

“I think the problems [with out car] are understood, the problem is that the solutions with what we see within our tools compared to what we’re seeing on track at the moment aren’t correlating and I think that’s what we need to get to the bottom of,” he said. [...] As McLaren's lead in both championships grows, there is considerable pressure on Horner and the team to resolve the issues as quickly as possible, given that the title challenge can slip away rapidly.

More News: Max Verstappen: RB21 'Too Slow' After Red Bull 'Struggle For Pace'

“I think you’ve got to understand where it’s weakness is and I think the problem that we have is that we’re at the end of a set of regulations where the gains are very very marginal and I think we’re seeing some of the shortcomings in our current tunnel that struggles in that area," he said.

“I think it’s clear we understand what the problem is, it’s implementing the solution.” [...] “Why can we not see within our tools what we’re seeing on the circuit and when you end up with a disconnect like that you have to obviously unpick it, we’ve got a strong technical team that have produced some amazing cars over the last few years and I’m confident they’ll get to the bottom of this issue."

> Bahrain Bulls 💙#F1 || #BahrainGP 🇧🇭 pic.twitter.com/3QfFOEXEHm
>
> — Oracle Red Bull Racing (@redbullracing) April 14, 2025

“But it’s literally the tool isn’t replicating with what we’re seeing on the track, and then it’s at that point it’s like telling the time on two different watches.”

Red Bull's wind tunnel issues trace back to the 2024 season, as upgrade packages failed to deliver the expected gains on track.
